title: Newsroom/health-reporter edition (deadline-friendly checklists)
context: Health journalists, science communicators, and health reporters are a high-leverage upstream audience: accurate coverage from this group reaches far more people than direct learner education. This backlog item adapts the core course content into a newsroom/deadline-friendly format — quick-reference checklists and worked examples that a journalist can apply before publishing a cancer-statistic story. Content is statistical-tier (medium risk) and reuses the core course content with a different format and audience framing. Biostatistician sign-off required for statistical correctness.
objective: Adapt the core content from all three courses into a newsroom/health-reporter edition: deadline-friendly checklists (e.g., 'before you publish a cancer survival story, check…') and a short worked-example set that a journalist can apply on deadline. Content must reuse the core course material (not diverge from it), implement all editorial guardrail rules, and be pitched at a health-reporter audience rather than a patient/public audience.
acceptanceCriteria: Produces deadline-friendly checklists for at least three reporting scenarios: covering a cancer survival statistic, covering a screening announcement, and covering a cancer rate trend
Each checklist is derived from the core course content and the misconception inventory (no divergence from the editorial guardrail rules)
Includes at least one short worked example showing how to apply a checklist to a real cancer news story (using cited aggregate data)
Written for a health-reporter audience: assumes numeracy, uses journalistic framing, is scannable on deadline
Carries 'not medical advice' framing (education-only) and does not imply screening recommendations
Biostatistician/epidemiologist sign-off on statistical correctness recorded in the reviewers ledger
output: A newsroom/health-reporter edition of the core content: deadline-friendly checklists for cancer-statistic reporting scenarios and a worked-example set — adapted from the core courses, stats-reviewer signed off, and targeted at a journalist audience.
